definitions: The saying of the same thing twice in different words, generally considered to be a fault of style.
A statement that is true by virtue of its logical form alone.
A phrase or expression in which the same thing is said twice using different words.
examples: The phrase 'free gift' is a tautology.
The statement 'It is either raining or it is not raining' is a tautology.
Avoid tautology in your writing for clarity.
